label: SES-013 close-out — entity schema-design conversation (workstream conversation #2 of 4)

session.title: entity schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #2
session.summary: Second of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream. Produced the entity schema specification (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md, 452 lines) defining the entity entity type for v2 storage under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Eight architectural questions resolved into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054). Inherited and applied without re-decision the two cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048). Registered entity_scopes_to_domain as the first formal cross-entity vocabulary entry in the methodology workstream — many-to-many via the universal references table. Two new planning items authored (PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel paired with PI-007 short codes; PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants and base-type/kind classification). Successor conversation: process schema design.
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation that produced PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md on 05-12-26. Second of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream established by SES-011. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `entity` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. The 'entity' here is the CRM-modeled noun — Contact, Account, Session, Engagement, Dues — the things the eventual CRM stores records about. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`. Ship thin in v0.4 — name, brief description, status — designed with growth path in mind for v0.5+ when Phase 3 demands attach fields, relationships, and full PRD content."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master, noting line 168 names ENT-NNN as the working assumption per DEC-043); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (predecessor spec, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (line 62 entity-definition scope; section 7.2 Domain Inventory output spec excluding candidate entities; section 4.3 between-sessions discipline); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from the domain conversation; payload exists but db-export snapshots through SES-011/DEC-043 confirm it has not yet been applied — numbers in this payload assume SES-012 lands first); decisions.json snapshot (DEC-038/039/043 read in detail for context on derived-fields, minimum-viable inventory rationale, identifier-asymmetry resolution).

Eight architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Affirmed ENT under domain.md's soft-3-letter posture (DEC-044). Three letters, unambiguous, no collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), matches v2 governance-entity norm, already named as the working assumption in DEC-043 and workstream plan line 168. → DEC-050.

Q2 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted a single content field (entity_description) rather than mirroring domain's two-content-field split (purpose + description). Reasoning: domain_purpose answers a priority-test mission question that has no analog for entity — an entity is a noun the client uses, and the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by its entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. Five substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: entity_identifier, entity_name, entity_description, entity_notes (optional), entity_status. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps (UI soft guidance only, matching domain). Case-insensitive entity_name uniqueness within the engagement. Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 without re-establishment. → DEC-051.

Q3 — Status lifecycle. Adopted domain's three-status pattern unchanged (candidate / confirmed / deferred), one-way propose-verify gate, freely bidirectional between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. Documented the entity-specific status-independence-from-affiliation-status posture: an entity's entity_status is its own field, set by the consultant based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from the statuses of the domains the entity scopes to. This independence matters because an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions (Contact in CBM might scope to MN/MR confirmed and FU deferred simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world). Server-side transition validation returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body, identical to domain. Noted as a future v0.5+ open question: a separate 'defined' or 'specified' status may surface once fields land, or the references-table cardinality between entity and field may suffice as the implicit signal. → DEC-052.

Q4 — Domain affiliation mechanism. The architecturally consequential question. Considered three candidates: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on entity table, (C) direct single-value FK. (C) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains (CBM Contact lives in MN/MR/FU simultaneously). (B) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema. Adopted (A). Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted (Phase 1 sometimes surfaces an entity name before its domain scoping is settled; the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation). Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. The existing ReferencesSection widget renders the affiliations in the detail pane for free. → DEC-053 (combines mechanism and vocabulary registration since they are inseparable architectural choices).

Q5 — Relationship-kind vocabulary additions. Single new vocab entry: entity_scopes_to_domain (source=entity, target=domain). Conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048 — naming convention adopted not re-decided. Three coordinated mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: add entry to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain}; Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. No other vocab additions in this spec; inbound kinds (process_touches_entity, field_belongs_to_entity, entity_variant_of_entity) are anticipated and documented informationally in section 3.3.2 but declared by their source-side specs (process.md and future v0.5+ specs), not by this one. → Folded into DEC-053.

Q6 — API surface. Adopted standard cross-spec endpoint set with no deviations (8 endpoints including /entities/next-identifier helper per DEC-043). Server-side status-transition validation identical to domain's pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling explicitly called out: domain affiliations are NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; the client makes separate POST /references calls with source/target/kind. This keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006) and matches how the v0.3 UI handles references for governance entities; the New dialog hides the two-call sequence behind a single user gesture but the API stays decomposed. No shortcut endpoints. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q7 — UI considerations. Adopted spec guide default ListDetailPanel layout with no architectural deviations. Methodology sidebar group position #2 (after Domains, before Processes and CRM Candidates which ship together in v0.4). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated). One real deferral: a Domains-column on the master pane was considered for v0.4 since entity-to-domain affiliation is the entity's defining classification, but deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes. Without short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance; with short codes (MN/MR/FU), the column becomes high-value. The N+1 / batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Create dialog's domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for v0.4-build planning to decide since both satisfy the acceptance criterion that the user can attach affiliations without leaving the UI. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q8 — Acceptance criteria. Drafted 16 numbered testable statements: 13 mirror domain's structural set (schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh) with entity field-name substitutions, plus three specifically covering the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism: (14) entity_scopes_to_domain registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and constrained correctly at _kinds_for_pair and refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint; (15) references created and queryable bidirectionally with persistence across soft-delete/restore of either endpoint; (16) sample CBM-redo Phase 1 records authored through the UI including domain affiliations. → Folded into DEC-054.

Outline approved and end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec committed to main at 40e2cf5. Consolidated eight question outcomes into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054) — narrower than domain's six because the cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048) carry forward unchanged and are adopted not re-decided in this spec.

Two new planning items authored: PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel (jointly enabled by PI-007 short codes); PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants (Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern; mechanism TBD) and base-type/kind classification (person / organization / event / transaction). Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups other deferred entity types) because these are entity-schema-growth, not new entity types.
session.artifacts_produced:
PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md (the entity methodology entity schema specification, v1.0, 452 lines). Second spec in the methodology-schema-specs/ directory introduced by SES-012.

Five decisions:
- DEC-050: entity identifier prefix and format
- DEC-051: entity field inventory and validation under minimum-viable v0.4 scope
- DEC-052: entity status lifecycle (adopts domain pattern; documents status-independent-of-affiliation-status posture)
- DEC-053: entity-to-domain affiliation mechanism via references-entity edge + entity_scopes_to_domain vocabulary registration
- DEC-054: entity API surface, UI defaults, deferred Domains-column posture, acceptance criteria for v0.4

Two new planning items:
- PI-009: master-pane Domains column on the Entities panel (paired with PI-007)
- PI-010: entity-schema v0.5+ extensions — variants and base-type/kind classification

This session record (SES-013).

Five decided_in references (DEC-050..DEC-054 → SES-013).

Git commits on crmbuilder main: 40e2cf5 (entity.md v1.0 initial draft + commit + push). Close-out payload commit forthcoming with this JSON file.
session.in_flight_at_end:
Next workstream conversation: process schema design. Kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-process.md. The process conversation inherits the conventions established by SES-012 and applied by this conversation:
- Parent-prefix field naming (process_name, process_status, etc.)
- {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming pattern (will likely declare process_belongs_to_domain as a direct FK column and process_touches_entity as a references-entity edge)
- Soft-3-letter prefix posture (likely PROC, justified in process.md section 3.1 if 4 letters is adopted)
- Default starter status candidate; rejection-via-soft-delete posture
- ListDetailPanel default UI layout under the Methodology sidebar group, position #3

The process conversation will also be the first to declare a relationship kind where entity is the inbound target (process_touches_entity working name). The cross-spec consistency check at the v0.4-build-planning conversation will validate that the source-side mechanism choice (likely references-entity edge given the many-to-many nature of process-to-entity relationships) aligns with this spec's section 3.3.2 anticipated-inbound table.

Open questions for the v0.4-build-planning conversation (accumulating across the workstream):
1. Spec guide section 6 amendments — reflect parent-prefix field naming and {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming conventions established by SES-012 and applied here.
2. PI-006 sizing — decide whether governance-entity field-name retrofit is in v0.4 scope or deferred to v0.5+ (carries from SES-012).
3. PI-008 sizing — decide whether the in-app inbox folder watcher ships in v0.4 (eliminating apply_close_out.py for future conversations) or defers to v0.5+ (carries from SES-012).
4. Create-dialog domain-affiliation flow for entity panel — create-then-attach vs create-with-attach (new this conversation; carries to v0.4 build).
5. Cross-spec consistency check on inbound vocabulary once process.md and crm_candidate.md land — verify no relationship-kind name collisions.
6. Concurrent identifier-assignment behavior for entity (likely same pattern as domain build adopts).

Workstream progress: 2 of 4 schema-design conversations complete (domain ✓, entity ✓). Process and crm_candidate schema-design conversations queued. v0.4-build-planning conversation queued after all four schema specs exist.

decisions.title: entity identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: The entity methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. The workstream plan line 168 and DEC-043 both name ENT-NNN as the working assumption. The predecessor domain.md established a soft-3-letter prefix posture: the v2 governance-entity norm is 3 letters (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA) and domain adopted DOM in keeping with that norm, but the spec guide allows 3-5 letters and downstream specs retain flexibility where 3 letters is ambiguous (e.g., PROC over PRC for clarity; CRMC over CRM to avoid product-name collision).
decisions.decision: Adopt ENT as the identifier prefix for the entity methodology entity. Format ENT-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission via the GET /entities/next-identifier helper (per DEC-043 / SES-010 resolution).
decisions.rationale: ENT is three letters, unambiguous, reads naturally as 'entity', has no collision with any existing prefix (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), and matches the existing v2 pattern visually. Adheres to domain.md's soft-3-letter posture without consuming any of the flexibility-budget the posture preserves for process and crm_candidate.
decisions.alternatives_considered: ENTITY (6 letters, verbose, breaks rhythm with existing prefixes). E (too short, ambiguous). EN (2 letters, breaks 3-5 convention). MENT for 'methodology entity' (verbose, unnecessary disambiguation since 'entity' is unambiguous in this context).
decisions.consequences: entity.md uses ENT-NNN throughout. No collision with existing prefixes. Downstream process and crm_candidate specs retain the soft-3-letter flexibility preserved by domain.md; each justifies its choice in its own section 3.1. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies no prefix collisions across the four methodology entity types.

decisions.title: entity field inventory and validation under minimum-viable v0.4 scope
decisions.context: DEC-039 explicitly justifies entity's inclusion in v0.4's minimum-viable inventory despite Phase 1 not formally producing an Entity Inventory output: 'real Phase 1 conversations naturally surface entity vocabulary (per the interview guide's line 62) and if v2 has no entity type those names land as loose nouns in session notes or process descriptions, forcing retrofit at the Phase 3 boundary.' DEC-039 names the working minimum as 'name, description, status'. The predecessor domain.md went further, splitting its content side into domain_purpose (the why — priority-test artifact) and domain_description (the what — kinds of work covered) plus optional domain_notes. The question is whether entity warrants the same split or simplifies to one description field.
decisions.decision: Adopt a five-field shape under the parent-prefix convention inherited from DEC-046: entity_identifier (TEXT, server-assigned, ^ENT-\d{3}$, unique), entity_name (TEXT, required, non-empty trimmed, case-insensitive unique within the engagement), entity_description (TEXT, required, non-empty trimmed, single content field rather than purpose+description split), entity_notes (TEXT, optional, plain text, internal consultant scratchpad), entity_status (TEXT, required, default candidate, enum). Plus inherited timestamp fields (entity_created_at, entity_updated_at, entity_deleted_at) — eight columns total. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps; UI placeholder text provides soft guidance only.
decisions.rationale: Single entity_description rather than a purpose+description split because domain_purpose exists to capture the priority-test mission-relationship at domain granularity ('why does the mission require this domain?'), and entity has no comparable mission relationship. An entity is a noun the client uses; the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by the entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would either duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. entity_notes captures the same consultant-scratchpad role as domain_notes — pattern-library rationale, push-back trails, between-session reasoning. No length caps because caps are easy to add via migration later and hard to relax once imposed; UI soft guidance handles the typical case. No entity_kind because base-type semantics are Phase 3 / CRM-engine territory; v0.4's job is to capture Phase 1 surfacing, not to model CRM implementation specifics.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Mirror domain's purpose+description split for cross-spec consistency (rejected as forcing a methodology concept that Phase 1 doesn't produce). Include entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (rejected as Phase 3 territory; tracked under PI-010). Storage-level length caps at v0.4 (rejected; caps are easy to add later and hard to relax once imposed; CBM-redo signal preferred for sizing). Markdown for entity_description (deferred to CBM-redo signal; matches domain's posture). Structured-journal pattern for entity_notes (deferred to CBM-redo signal; matches domain_notes posture).
decisions.consequences: Eight-column entities table. CBM redo's Phase 1 captures entity records with the field set as specified; if real use surfaces methodology gaps (entity_purpose-shaped need, base-type classification need, length pathologies, markdown rendering pressure), they become v0.5+ candidates with concrete real-use evidence. Cross-spec naming convention from DEC-046 applied uniformly: even entity_identifier and timestamps carry the entity_ prefix for full convention consistency.

decisions.title: entity status lifecycle (adopts domain pattern; documents status-independent-of-affiliation-status posture)
decisions.context: DEC-047 established for domain a three-value lifecycle (candidate / confirmed / deferred) with default candidate starter, one-way propose-verify gate (no regression from confirmed or deferred back to candidate), bidirectional movement between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. The cross-spec principle 'status values track engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' was established. The question for entity is whether to mirror domain's lifecycle exactly or deviate, and whether entity-specific status semantics need additional documentation.
decisions.decision: Adopt domain's three-value lifecycle unchanged. entity_status enum values candidate / confirmed / deferred with identical predecessor/successor transition rules. Default candidate on POST omission; explicit non-starter values permitted on POST for bulk-import scenarios. Server-side transition validation at the access layer returns HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition envelope on bad transitions. Rejection via soft-delete; no archived status. Add one entity-specific subsection (3.4.3) documenting that entity_status is independent of any affiliated-domain statuses — an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions and its own status is set by consultant judgment based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from references.
decisions.rationale: Entities, like domains, are surfaced by the consultant and verified by the client; the propose-verify dynamic is the same, so the lifecycle pattern transfers without modification. The status-independence-from-affiliation-status subsection matters because the temptation to derive entity status from the union of its domain affiliations' statuses is real but wrong: Contact in CBM might scope to MN (confirmed), MR (confirmed), FU (deferred) simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world. Coupling the two would hide that client decision behind a derivation rule. Explicit documentation prevents either (a) future maintainers from introducing the derivation, or (b) downstream specs from inferring different semantics.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Add a fourth status value 'defined' or 'specified' to distinguish 'client agreed entity is in scope' from 'we have a full definition of this entity' (rejected for v0.4; v0.4 has no fields and the distinction has no expressive shape; revisited as a CBM-redo open question for v0.5+). Derive entity_status from the union of its affiliated-domain statuses (rejected as documented in section 3.4.3). Different transition rules from domain's (no reason to deviate surfaced).
decisions.consequences: entity.md section 3.4 mirrors domain.md section 3.4 structurally with one additional subsection (3.4.3) on status independence. Cross-spec principle 'status tracks engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' applied consistently across domain and entity. CBM redo will surface whether the 'defined vs confirmed' distinction is needed for v0.5+; if so, either a fourth status value or the implicit signal from entity-to-field references' presence becomes the mechanism.

decisions.title: entity-to-domain affiliation mechanism via references-entity edge + entity_scopes_to_domain vocabulary registration
decisions.context: domain.md section 3.3.2 named many-to-many via the references entity as the working assumption for entity-to-domain affiliation, citing CBM's Contact-shared-across-MN/MR/FU pattern. The mechanism choice belongs to entity.md as the source-side spec. Three candidate mechanisms exist in v2: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on the entity table, (C) direct single-value FK on the entity table. The references infrastructure (DEC-006) is the established cross-entity-type edge store; the question is whether to use it for entity-to-domain or to introduce a new pattern.
decisions.decision: Adopt option A: references-entity edge with new relationship kind entity_scopes_to_domain. Mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: (1) add entity_scopes_to_domain to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; (2) extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain} (the only kind for this pair in v0.4); (3) Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted in v0.4. Source must be a live entity record; target must be a live domain record (existing access-layer rules). Duplicate (source_id, target_id, relationship_kind) tuples rejected by the references-table uniqueness constraint. Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. Vocabulary entry conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048.
decisions.rationale: Option C (direct single-value FK) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains. Option B (multi-value FK on entity table) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries ('what entities scope to this domain?') require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism already handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema for what the references entity exists to absorb. Option A reuses existing v2 infrastructure (refs table, vocab.py, Alembic migration pattern, ReferencesSection widget) and matches how the v0.3 desktop UI handles references for governance entities. Zero-affiliation permitted because Phase 1 sometimes surfaces entity names before domain scoping is settled and the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B (multi-value FK column on entity, e.g., entity_domain_identifiers as JSON array). Option C (direct single-value entity_domain_identifier FK). Hybrid pattern with one 'primary domain' FK plus references for additional affiliations (rejected as inventing complexity without value — CBM's Contact doesn't have a primary domain, it's genuinely shared). Different relationship-kind naming (e.g., target-first 'domain_contains_entity', or generic 'scopes_to'; rejected per DEC-048's source-first convention and disambiguation benefit of explicit source/target naming).
decisions.consequences: Refs table grows by the count of entity-to-domain edges across the engagement. ReferencesSection widget renders affiliations in the entity detail pane for free; same widget renders inbound entity references from the domain side. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies the source-side mechanism choices align across the four methodology entity types. The choice establishes a pattern for downstream specs: when a methodology entity has a many-to-many relationship to another methodology entity, the references-entity edge is the default mechanism unless there's a substantive reason to deviate.

decisions.title: entity API surface, UI defaults, deferred Domains-column posture, acceptance criteria for v0.4
decisions.context: domain.md adopted the spec guide's default 8-endpoint API surface and the default ListDetailPanel UI layout without deviations. For entity, the same defaults fit cleanly, but two entity-specific UI questions need explicit decisions: (a) whether to add a Domains column to the master pane (entity-to-domain affiliation is entity's defining classification in v0.4), and (b) what the create-dialog domain-affiliation flow looks like (create-then-attach via the detail pane vs create-with-attach via a multi-select in the dialog itself).
decisions.decision: Adopt standard 8-endpoint API surface with no deviations: GET /entities, GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /entities/{id}, POST /entities, POST /entities/{id}/restore, GET /entities/next-identifier. Server-side status-transition validation per the domain pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling: domain affiliations NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; client makes separate POST /references calls. Default ListDetailPanel UI in the existing Methodology sidebar group (position #2 after Domains). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated) — defer a Domains column to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes, tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Edge-text confirmation on delete. Create-dialog domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for the v0.4-build-planning conversation to decide. 16 testable acceptance criteria covering schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh, plus three specific to the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism.
decisions.rationale: No-deviation default endpoints minimize cross-spec complexity and reuse existing v2 patterns. Decomposed reference handling keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006); shortcut endpoints would invent API surface area that the references-table API already covers. Domains column deferred to v0.5+ for two reasons: (a) without PI-007 short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance, so the column's UX value is tied to PI-007 landing; (b) the batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Shipping the column alongside PI-007 keeps both deliverables aligned. Create-dialog flow left open because both candidate patterns satisfy the acceptance criterion that the user can attach affiliations without leaving the UI; the choice is UI-implementation, not schema-design, and the v0.4-build-planning conversation has the right cross-cutting visibility to decide.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Ship the Domains column in v0.4 rendering raw DOM-NNN identifiers (rejected as poor UX without short codes; better to ship the column alongside PI-007). Decide the create-dialog flow inline in this spec (rejected as UI-implementation question better answered with cross-cutting v0.4-build visibility). Inline-affiliation endpoint as a convenience shortcut (rejected as inventing API surface that references-table API already covers; decomposed pattern is more consistent). Additional server-side list filters in v0.4 (deferred to CBM-redo signal; client-side filtering is sufficient at the expected entity count). Different sidebar position for Entities (no reason to deviate from workstream-order surfaced).
decisions.consequences: entity.md sections 3.5 / 3.6 / 3.7 follow the established patterns with explicit calls on the deferred Domains column and open create-dialog flow. v0.4-build-planning conversation has to decide the create-dialog flow as part of UI implementation. CBM redo will validate (a) whether the Domains-column deferral is right (low-friction without it, or genuine pain point); (b) whichever create-dialog flow is implemented (is the create-then-attach two-gesture sequence acceptable, or is multi-select-on-create needed). PI-009 added as a v0.5+ planning item. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies sidebar ordering and panel layout patterns align across all four methodology entity types.

planning_items.title: Master-pane Domains column on the Entities panel (paired with PI-007 short codes)
planning_items.description: DEC-054 defers a Domains column on the master pane of the Entities panel to v0.5+, jointly enabled by PI-007 (domain.short_code field). The column renders affiliated domains as comma-separated short codes (e.g., 'MN, MR, FU') once short codes exist; without them, DOM-NNN identifiers don't tell a consultant anything at a glance and the column's UX value is lost. Implementation scope when both PI-007 and PI-009 land together: (a) access-layer batched-join query (list_entities_with_affiliations() or equivalent) returning each entity's affiliated-domain short codes alongside its scalar fields without N+1 queries; (b) ListDetailPanel view-model update to surface the new column; (c) column-width and sorting behavior decisions (sort by what? probably alphabetical-by-short-code-list with secondary sort by entity_name). Prioritization signal: CBM redo will reveal whether the absence of the column in v0.4 is high-friction (do consultants frequently click into the detail pane to check affiliations? do they request a list view grouped by domain?). If high-friction, PI-009 + PI-007 move up in v0.5 priority; if low-friction, they stay queued.

planning_items.title: Entity-schema v0.5+ extensions — variants and base-type/kind classification
planning_items.description: DEC-051 and DEC-053 jointly defer two entity-schema growth areas to v0.5+: (a) entity variants — the Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern from CBM history where two related-but-distinct entities share a base type but carry different fields and role semantics; (b) entity base-type / kind classification — person / organization / event / transaction-style categorization that informs CRM-engine selection and field-shape defaults. Both are intentionally absent from v0.4: variants because Phase 1 doesn't produce them and v0.4 has no fields to vary across them; base-type/kind because it's Phase 3 territory tied to CRM-engine specifics that v0.4's thin shape stays neutral about. Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec as new methodology entity types) because PI-010 is entity-schema growth — extending the existing entity type — not introducing new entity types. Variant mechanism TBD: candidates include self-referential FK (entity_parent_identifier), references-entity edge (entity_variant_of_entity), or a separate variant entity type sitting between base entity and field. Mechanism choice is a v0.5+ design conversation. Workaround in v0.4: variants are distinguished by suffixing entity_name ('Contact — Mentor', 'Contact — Client'); CBM redo will reveal how much friction the workaround creates and inform mechanism design. Base-type/kind classification: candidate v0.5+ shape is an enum field (entity_kind: person | organization | event | transaction | other) on the entity table, with validation that informs Phase 3 field-shape defaults and Phase 5 CRM-evaluation scoring.
